title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Runtime Lens: Normalization Form
summary: Visually identical words count separately, compatibility characters collapse without disclosure, lowercasing misses caseless matches, and the report cannot link normalized tokens back to original text. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the normalization form increment in versioned reversible Unicode policy. Le…
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_unicode_normalization_2(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-normalization-form-i77reh
# Competency: bookbot-normalization-form.
# Case 5037: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_normalization_form_i77reh(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_i77reh = "i77reh-her77i-5037"
    assert evidence_variant_i77reh
    evidence_axes_i77reh = (
        "corpusi77reh",
        "policyher77i",
        "revision5037",
        "commandi77reh5037",
        "reporther77i5037",
        "test5037i77reh",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_i77reh)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Normalization Form model · Theory Normalization Form, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: NFKC preserves every distinction that readers and identifiers may need.
